lab 3: vectors and dataframes
use logical operators inside vector brackets
tibble to create a dataframe
$ to access a column

lab 4: plots

lab 5:
select()
can be used with contains, starts_with, ends_with, where() inside
rename()
filter()
arrange()

lab 6:
`distinct()` to find unique observations in rows.    
`mutate()` to create new columns from existing columns.    
`mutate()` with `across` and `where` to transform multiple columns that meet specific criteria.
`if_else()`

lab 7+8: summarize
`summarize()` can be used to produce quick statistical summaries
used with mean, median, n, 
group_by()
  group_by(years_of_sample_collection, sex) %>% 
  summarize(mean_estimated_age_years=mean(estimated_age_years, na.rm=T),...
n_distinct used inside summarize
pull() for extracting a column
count() used outside summarize
count(vore, sort=T)

lab 9: pivots
`Tidy` data follows three conventions:   
(1) each variable has its own column  
(2) each observation has its own row  
(3) each value has its own cell  
`pivot_longer`(cols, names_to, values_to)
separate(patient, into=c("patient", "gender"), sep="_")
unite(patient, "patient", "gender", sep="_")
`pivot_wider`(names_from, values_from)

lab 10: plots
geom_bar and geom_col (x and y defined) for counts
scatter for 2 cont var
boxplot for range


lab 11: plots
line plots for changes over time geom_line
histograms and density for distribution geom_histogram geom_density
use case_when to create new variables from others, used in a mutate function
facet_wrap or facet_grid for multiple panels (x~y)

lab 13: 
handling NA's
checking for NA's:
summarize(across(everything(), ~ sum(is.na(.)))) or ~~sum(.==-999)
change values to NA's:
mutate(max_life=na_if(max_life, 0))
library naniar:
miss_var_summary(life_history_no_nas)
gg_miss_var()
replace_with_na(replace = list...
joining tables:
left_join, right_join, full_join
library lubridate:
`ymd`, `mdy`, `dmy`, `mdy_hms`, `ymd_hms`

lab 14: mapping
stadia->key
library ggmap: 
create lat and long vectors with min and max
create bbox
create map with get_stadiamap(bbox, maptype="stamen_terrain", zoom=7)
ggmap(map)
library leaflet:
leaflet(dataset) %>% 
  addTiles() %>% 
  addProviderTiles(providers$Stadia.StamenTerrain) %>% 
  addCircleMarkers(
    lng = ~longitude,
    lat = ~latitude,
    radius=2,
    stroke=F,
    fillOpacity=0.7
  ) %>% 
  addScaleBar(position="bottomleft") %>% 
  fitBounds(lng1=lon_min, lat1=lat_min,
            lng2=lon_max, lat2=lat_max)

lab 15: shiny app
ui <- fluidPage(
  
)

server <- function(input, output, session) {
  
}

shinyApp(ui, server)

example: 
ui <- dashboardPage(
    dashboardHeader(title="Sex Composition of Wolves By Population"),
    
    dashboardSidebar(
      
      selectInput("x",
                  "Select Population",
                  choices=sort(unique(wolves$pop)),
                  selected="AK.PEN")
    ),
    
    dashboardBody(
      plotOutput("plot", width="500px", height="400px")
    )
)

server <- function(input, output, session) {
  output$plot <- renderPlot({
    wolves %>% 
      filter(sex!="NA") %>% 
      filter(input$x==.data[["pop"]]) %>% 
      ggplot(aes(x=sex, fill=sex))+
      geom_bar(color="black",
               alpha=0.8)+
      theme_gray()+
      labs(x="Sex")
  })
}

shinyApp(ui, server)
